entries.summary: OpenAI's board fired CEO Sam Altman, citing a loss of confidence in his leadership.
President Greg Brockman resigned hours after Altman's removal in solidarity.
OpenAI named chief technology officer Mira Murati as interim chief executive.
OpenAI investors pressed the board to reverse course and reinstate Altman.
Talks to bring Altman back stalled into Sunday night without an agreement.
Microsoft announced Altman and Brockman would lead a new advanced AI research group.
Twitch co-founder Emmett Shear was appointed OpenAI's new interim chief executive.
More than seven hundred OpenAI employees threatened to quit unless directors resigned.
OpenAI announced Altman's return as chief executive under a reconstituted initial board.
Reuters reported staff researchers had warned the board about a project called Q*.
Altman officially resumed duties as OpenAI's chief executive following the deal.
OpenAI added Microsoft as a non-voting board observer under the new structure.
An internal review found the board's prior decision to remove Altman was unjustified.
OpenAI added three new directors, including former Sony executive Nicole Seligman.
Co-founder Ilya Sutskever announced he was leaving OpenAI after nearly a decade.
Superalignment co-lead Jan Leike resigned, saying safety had taken a back seat.
Chief technology officer Mira Murati announced her departure from OpenAI.
OpenAI closed a 6.6-billion-dollar funding round at a 157-billion-dollar valuation.
OpenAI announced its board had ousted Sam Altman, saying he was not consistently candid.
Greg Brockman, removed as chair, said he was quitting OpenAI hours later.
OpenAI named Mira Murati interim chief executive while it searched for a permanent replacement.
Altman discussed launching a rival venture as investors pushed for his return.
Negotiations to reinstall Altman as CEO collapsed late Sunday after board resistance.
Microsoft chief Satya Nadella said Altman and Brockman would join Microsoft to lead AI.
Nearly all OpenAI staff signed a letter demanding the board resign or face mass departures.
Ilya Sutskever publicly said he regretted his role in the board's removal of Altman.
OpenAI said Altman would return as CEO under a new three-member transitional board.
Reports surfaced that researchers had flagged a powerful project named Q* before Altman's firing.
Altman formally resumed his role as OpenAI chief, with Microsoft taking a board observer seat.
An OpenAI-commissioned WilmerHale review concluded Altman's conduct did not warrant removal.
Chief scientist Ilya Sutskever said he was leaving OpenAI to pursue a personal project.
Jan Leike said OpenAI's safety culture had taken a back seat to product priorities.
Mira Murati announced she was stepping down to do her own exploration.
OpenAI raised 6.6 billion dollars at a 157-billion-dollar valuation, its largest round.
